ddls:
CREATE TABLE "publications" (
  "publication_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each publication */,
  "bibliography_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the bibliographic entry */,
  "publication_type" TEXT /* Type of the publication (e.g., Article, Synthesis, MastersThesis) */,
  "key" TEXT /* Unique key for the publication */,
  "bibtype" TEXT /* Type of bibliographic entry */,
  "doi" TEXT /* Digital Object Identifier for the publication */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL for accessing the publication online */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of publication */,
  "publisher" TEXT /* Publisher of the publication */,
  "volume" INTEGER /* Volume of the journal (if applicable) */,
  "number" INTEGER /* Issue number of the journal (if applicable) */,
  "author" TEXT /* Authors of the publication */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the publication */,
  "journal" TEXT /* Journal name (if applicable) */,
  "school" TEXT /* School associated with the publication (if applicable) */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution associated with the publication (if applicable) */,
  "abstract" TEXT /* Abstract of the publication */,
  "keywords" TEXT /* Keywords for the publication */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the publication (e.g., published, in press, submitted) */,
  "license" TEXT /* License or copyright information for the publication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("publication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_publications_school FOREIGN KEY ("school") REFERENCES schools ("school_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_publications_institution FOREIGN KEY ("institution") REFERENCES institutions ("institution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_publications_publication_type FOREIGN KEY ("publication_type") REFERENCES publication_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_publications_publisher FOREIGN KEY ("publisher") REFERENCES publishers ("publisher_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_publications_journal FOREIGN KEY ("journal") REFERENCES journals ("journal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "publication_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the publication associated with the dataset */,
  "dataset_version" TEXT /* Version of the dataset */,
  "repository_url" TEXT /* URL for the dataset repository */,
  "documentation_url" TEXT /* URL for the dataset documentation */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "keywords" TEXT /* Keywords for the dataset */,
  "format" TEXT /* Format of the dataset (e.g., CSV, JSON, Excel) */,
  "size" TEXT /* Size of the dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_datasets_publication_id FOREIGN KEY ("publication_id") REFERENCES publications ("publication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "author_name" TEXT /* Full name of the author */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliation of the author */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biography of the author */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "publication_authors" (
  "publication_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the publication */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the author */,
  "author_order" INTEGER /* Order of the author in the publication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("publication_id", "author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "publication_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each publication type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the publication type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the publication type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bibliographic_entries" (
  "bibliography_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the bibliographic entry */,
  "entry_type" TEXT /* Type of the bibliographic entry */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the bibliographic entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bibliography_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "publishers" (
  "publisher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each publisher */,
  "publisher_name" TEXT /* Name of the publisher */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the publisher */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the publisher */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("publisher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "journals" (
  "journal_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each journal */,
  "journal_name" TEXT /* Name of the journal */,
  "publisher_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the publisher of the journal */,
  "issn" TEXT /* International Standard Serial Number for the journal */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("journal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "schools" (
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each school */,
  "school_name" TEXT /* Name of the school */,
  "institution_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the institution the school belongs to */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the school */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "institutions" (
  "institution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each institution */,
  "institution_name" TEXT /* Name of the institution */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the institution */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the institution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("institution_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "profile" TEXT /* Additional information about the user (e.g., research interests, expertise) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "publication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the publication being accessed */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the publication or dataset */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the publication or dataset was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location from which the access event occurred */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_publication_id FOREIGN KEY ("publication_id") REFERENCES publications ("publication_id")
)
